title: UPDATED: U.S., Israel Launch Strikes Against Iran, Tehran Retaliates Across Region

text:
This post has been updated with a statement from U.S. Central Command, the Israeli Defense Forces, a map with confirmed Iranian retaliation strikes and information on the death of Iran’s sectarian leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
U.S. and Israeli forces kicked off an ongoing strike campaign against targets in Iran on Saturday, President Donald Trump announced in the early morning.
The U.S. Operation Epic Fury and Israeli Operation Raging Lion struck at Iranian leadership and military targets across the country, launching from both sea and land airbases, according to multiple reports.
Late Saturday afternoon, Trump announced on social media that the strikes killed Iran’s sectarian leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
“Khamenei, one of the most evil people in History, is dead. This is not only Justice for the people of Iran, but for all Great Americans, and those people from many Countries throughout the World, that have been killed or mutilated by Khamenei and his gang of bloodthirsty THUGS,” Trump wrote.
Satellite photos showed the residence of Khamenei destroyed. Iranian state media confirmed the death late Saturday night.
The attacks began around 1:15 a.m. Eastern Standard Time, according to a Saturday afternoon statement from U.S. Central Command.
“Targets included Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps command and control facilities, Iranian air defense capabilities, missile and drone launch sites, and military airfields,” reads the statement shared on social media. “The first hours of the operation included precision munitions launched from air, land and sea. Additionally, CENTCOM’s Task Force Scorpion Strike employed low-cost one-way attack drones for the first time in combat.”
The CENTCOM video and photos released this afternoon show Marine F-35C Lighting II and Navy F/A-18E/F Super Hornet fighters launched from aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) in the North Arabian Sea. In addition, CENTCOM published photos and video of U.S. guided-missile destroyers launching Tomahawk land attack missiles.
Israel has released video of their forces striking targets in Western Iran, claiming 11 sites in a graphic published on social media. Satellite photo company Vantor released photos of the strike aftermath at the Iranian naval base at Konarak, showing at least one vessel burning at the pier and damage to an airfield.
“Our objective is to defend the American people by eliminating imminent threats from the Iranian regime,” Trump said in prerecorded remarks posted on White House social media accounts early Saturday morning. “The regime’s proxies have continued to launch countless attacks against American forces stationed in the Middle East in recent years, as well as U.S. naval and commercial vessels in international shipping lanes. It’s been mass terror, and we’re not going to put up with it any longer.”
Following the speech, Trump told The Washington Post in a brief phone interview from Mar-a-Lago that his primary objective was “freedom for the people” of Iran. “We’re doing this for the future, and it is a noble mission,” Trump said, as reported by The Washington Post.
In his prerecorded address, Trump said the military objectives included targeting the country’s military manufacturing capability.
“We are going to destroy their missiles and raze their missile industry to the ground. It will be totally, again, obliterated. We are going to annihilate their navy. We are going to ensure that the region’s terrorist proxies can no longer destabilize the region or the world and attack our forces,” Trump said. “We will ensure that Iran does not obtain a nuclear weapon. It’s a very simple message.”
The joint attacks followed a third round of negotiations earlier this week between Tehran and Washington over Iran’s nuclear program.
USNI News map made with the assistance of an artificial intelligence agent
Trump warned that casualties among American service members could come as part of Iran’s retaliation. Bahraini officials reported that Iranian missiles struck the U.S. 5th Fleet headquarters in Manama, Bahrain, confirming several posts on social media. U.S. officials have not reported any casualties as of publication. A statement from U.S. Central Command following an earlier version of this post said there were no casualties.
Pentagon and Navy spokespeople referred USNI News to Trump’s statement when contacted Saturday.
Local reporters from Stars and Stripes reported the Iranian retaliation attacks have continued for hours in Manama.
An Iranian attack drone also struck the Naval Support Activity on Saturday, seemingly targeting and destroying a radome, based on videos shared on social media. The drone appeared to be a Shahed-type unmanned aerial combat vehicle, a mainstay of Tehran’s regional strike capabilities that has accelerated American counter-unmanned aerial system efforts for troops based in U.S. Central Command. Iran has targeted U.S. satellite communication nodes in previous strikes. During the 12-day war last year, Iranian missiles damaged an Al-Udeid Air Base facility containing a secure satellite communication dish.
Other retaliation strikes have been reported across the region against U.S. and Israeli targets in the UAE, Israel, Jordan, Kuwait and Qatar. U.S. embassies in the region have ordered shelter-in-place advisories.
The strikes on U.S. targets in the other countries may signal Iran is attempting to pressure the Gulf Cooperation Council countries, Middle East expert Mohammed Albasha, who writes the risk analysis Basha Report, told USNI News. That strategy could backfire, he said.
“This is an extremely dangerous moment. The risk of escalation is real, including the possibility that both sides begin targeting energy facilities across the Gulf. The phone call between Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed, despite recent tensions between Riyadh and Abu Dhabi, shows that Iran’s actions are bringing Arab Gulf states closer together,” Albasha said.
Iranian proxy groups in Iraq are likely the first that will attack U.S. assets, according to Albasha. The Houthis, an Iranian proxy group in Yemen, issued messages of support for Iran following the strikes but have not yet committed to any action. The group previously attacked commercial ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden in support of Gaza.
Pentagon and Navy spokespeople referred USNI News to Trump’s statement when contacted on Saturday morning. A White House pool report said there would be no additional statements Saturday from Trump.
Tankers are still moving through the Strait of Hormuz, though Iran announced a ban on vessel traffic, Campbell University professor and maritime expert Sal Mercogliano wrote on X Saturday morning.
U.S. naval and air forces have massed in the region over the last several weeks. The Lincoln Carrier Strike Group has been operating in the North Arabian Sea, while the Gerald R. Ford CSG is in the Eastern Mediterranean. There were 14 guided-missile destroyers in the region between the two strike groups and independent deployers, stretched across the Mediterranean and the Middle East.
The U.S. destroyers in the region are armed with Standard Missile 3s – which can intercept ballistic missiles – as well as SM-2s and SM-6s that counter traditional air threats like cruise missiles and attack drones. Additionally, the U.S. has other ground-based missile defense assets in the region, including U.S. Army Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) Systems and Army Patriot anti-air PAC-3 missile batteries.
The attacks come eight months after the U.S. struck several Iranian nuclear sites using bunker buster bombs last June. That operation, known as Midnight Hammer, included B-2 bombers and one unnamed submarine launching Tomahawk missiles. At the time, Trump said Iran’s nuclear enrichment sites had “been completely and totally obliterated.”
In concluding his prerecorded remarks, Trump pressed the Iranian people to overthrow their government.
“The hour of your freedom is at hand. Stay sheltered. Don’t leave your home. It’s very dangerous outside,” he said. “Bombs will be dropping everywhere. When we are finished, take over your government. It will be yours to take. This will be probably your only chance for generations.”
USNI News staff writers Mallory Shelbourne, Caitlyn Burchett, Heather Mongilio, Sam LaGrone and USNI News contributor Aaron-Matthew Lariosa compiled this report. USNI News AI use in this article is strictly confined to assistance with creating code to create display maps and was not used to generate captions or imagery.
